what_it_does: Declares the 'resolution' axis as a module-level AXIS dict — the detail/grain axis (how close). Defines id and namespace 'resolution', a single discrete field 'grain' (coarse | medium | fine, the MRL grain / levels-of-detail), value_source 'live', and a description stating it is ONE axis with two facets resolved 2026-06-22: (a) extraction-time forward-stepping (coarse on all, step deeper in-pass per chunk by its coarse output's kind, forward-only, never re-extract), and (b) read-time multi-scale rollup (subset projection of a stored superset). It unifies the dragnet grain axis and multi-scale embeddings as one axis.
observations: Pure declarative data — only the AXIS dict, no logic
Single discrete field: {'grain': 'discrete'} with values coarse | medium | fine
desc records a dated design resolution (2026-06-22, 'Tim's STEPPED cascade')
Distinguishes two facets: extraction-time forward-stepping vs read-time multi-scale rollup
Extraction rule: coarse on all, step deeper per chunk by its own coarse output's kind (decision/spec/discussion to fine; log stops), forward-only, never re-extract, no up-projection
Read facet: a subset projection of the stored superset (determine-many)
Cites the recollection dragnet grain axis commit 4207e75 and unifies it with multi-scale embeddings
standouts: Collapses two previously separate concepts (dragnet grain axis + multi-scale embeddings) into ONE axis with two facets
Encodes a kind-driven forward-stepping extraction policy (decision/spec to fine; log stops) directly in the axis description
conventions: Follows the same AXIS-dict declaration pattern as register.py (id, namespace, fields, value_source, desc)
Uses the desc field to carry substantial dated design rationale, not just a definition
concerns: The desc field is dense with design history and policy; the actual stepping/rollup logic lives elsewhere, so this declaration alone does not enforce the stated invariants
notes: The grain/level-of-detail axis declaration — one discrete grain field unifying extraction-time forward-stepping and read-time multi-scale rollup under a never-re-extract rule.
questions: Where is the extraction-time forward-stepping logic (kind-based deepening) and the read-time rollup actually implemented?
What enforces the forward-only / never-re-extract invariant declared here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header and desc describe the resolution/grain axis and its two facets; the dict declares exactly a discrete grain field, with the policy detail carried in desc.
intention_signals: To define resolution as a single live axis that unifies how deeply content is extracted (stepped forward by kind, once) and how it is read back (multi-scale rollup from a stored superset), preserving the never-re-extract invariant.
novelty: The explicit one-axis-two-facets resolution and the kind-driven forward-only stepping cascade are distinctive, and the dict embeds a dated design decision as its rationale.
contribution: Contributes the 'resolution' axis definition: a discrete live grain field unifying extraction-time forward-stepping and read-time multi-scale rollup under never-re-extract.
summary_for_embedding: An axes declaration file defining the discrete live 'resolution' (grain) axis unifying extraction-time forward-stepping and read-time multi-scale rollup under a never-re-extract rule.
intention_for_embedding: To declare resolution as one live axis covering both how deeply content is extracted (once, forward) and how it is read back at multiple scales.
